- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. FROM debian:trixie-slim

ARG RTK_VERSION=0.42.4
ARG CODEX_VERSION=0.141.0
ARG GIT_WT_VERSION=0.29.0
ARG AGENT_BROWSER_VERSION=0.29.0
ARG TARGETARCH

ENV LANG=C.UTF-8
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# System packages
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    build-essential \
    ca-certificates \
    curl \
    git \
    grep \
    jq \
    locales \
    openssh-client \
    ripgrep \
    tree \
    unzip \
    zsh \
    zsh-autosuggestions \
    zsh-syntax-highlighting \
    && sed -i 's/^# *\(C.UTF-8\)/\1/' /etc/locale.gen \
    && locale-gen \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# GitHub CLI + Docker CLI
RUN curl -fsSL https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
      -o /usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
    && echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg] https://cli.github.com/packages stable main" \
      > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/github-cli.list \
    && curl -fsSL https://download.docker.com/linux/debian/gpg \
      -o /usr/share/keyrings/docker-archive-keyring.gpg \
    && echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/docker-archive-keyring.gpg] https://download.docker.com/linux/debian trixie stable" \
      > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/docker.list \
    && apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends gh docker-ce-cli docker-compose-plugin \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# starship prompt
RUN curl -fsSL https://starship.rs/install.sh | sh -s -- -y

# RTK
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) RTK_PKG="rtk_${RTK_VERSION}-1_amd64.deb" ;; \
      arm64) RTK_PKG="rtk-aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk/releases/download/v${RTK_VERSION}/${RTK_PKG}" -o "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}" \
    && if [ "${TARGETARCH}" = "amd64" ]; then \
         dpkg -i "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}"; \
       else \
         tar xzf "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}" -C /usr/local/bin; \
       fi \
    && rm -f "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}"

# agent-browser
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) AB_ARCH="x64" ;; \
      arm64) AB_ARCH="arm64" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser/releases/download/v${AGENT_BROWSER_VERSION}/agent-browser-linux-${AB_ARCH}" \
      -o /usr/local/bin/agent-browser \

ARG TARGETARCH

    build-essential \

    locales \

    ripgrep \
    tree \
    unzip \
    zsh \

    && sed -i 's/^# *\(C.UTF-8\)/\1/' /etc/locale.gen \
    && locale-gen \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# GitHub CLI + Docker CLI
RUN curl -fsSL https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
      -o /usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
    && echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg] https://cli.github.com/packages stable main" \
      > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/github-cli.list \
    && curl -fsSL https://download.docker.com/linux/debian/gpg \
      -o /usr/share/keyrings/docker-archive-keyring.gpg \
    && echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/docker-archive-keyring.gpg] https://download.docker.com/linux/debian trixie stable" \
      > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/docker.list \
    && apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends gh docker-ce-cli docker-compose-plugin \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# starship prompt
RUN curl -fsSL https://starship.rs/install.sh | sh -s -- -y

# RTK
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) RTK_PKG="rtk_${RTK_VERSION}-1_amd64.deb" ;; \
      arm64) RTK_PKG="rtk-aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk/releases/download/v${RTK_VERSION}/${RTK_PKG}" -o "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}" \
    && if [ "${TARGETARCH}" = "amd64" ]; then \
         dpkg -i "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}"; \
       else \
         tar xzf "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}" -C /usr/local/bin; \
       fi \
    && rm -f "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}"

# agent-browser
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) AB_ARCH="x64" ;; \
      arm64) AB_ARCH="arm64" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser/releases/download/v${AGENT_BROWSER_VERSION}/agent-browser-linux-${AB_ARCH}" \
      -o /usr/local/bin/agent-browser \

RUN curl -fsSL "https://github.com/k1LoW/git-wt/releases/download/v${GIT_WT_VERSION}/git-wt_${GIT_WT_VERSION}-1_${TARGETARCH}.deb" \
      -o /tmp/git-wt.deb \

    && rm /tmp/git-wt.deb

# Scripts

# Non-root user

    && useradd -m -s /bin/zsh agent

ENV PATH="/home/agent/.local/bin:${PATH}"

# Claude Code

# entire (AI session capture for git)
RUN curl -fsSL https://entire.io/install.sh | bash

# Codex
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) CODEX_ARCH="x86_64" ;; \
      arm64) CODEX_ARCH="aarch64" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/openai/codex/releases/download/rust-v${CODEX_VERSION}/codex-${CODEX_ARCH}-unknown-linux-musl.tar.gz" \

    && rm /tmp/codex.tar.gz

USER root
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends python3 python3-pip \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
USER agent
RUN pip3 install --user --break-system-packages apm-cli

RUN rtk init -g && rtk init -g --codex

WORKDIR /workspace
CMD ["sleep", "infinity"]

```bash
rtk git status          # Compact status

```

Note: Git passthrough works for ALL subcommands, even those not explicitly listed.

### GitHub (26-87% savings)

file.content:
name: Build and Push

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - docker/**
      - compose.yml
      - .github/workflows/build-push.yml
  workflow_dispatch:

env:
  REGISTRY: ghcr.io
  IMAGE_NAME: syati/agent-stack

jobs:
  build:
    strategy:
      matrix:
        include:
          - runner: ubuntu-latest
            platform: linux/amd64
          - runner: ubuntu-24.04-arm
            platform: linux/arm64
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3

      - uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
        id: meta
        with:
          images: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}

      - uses: docker/build-push-action@v6
        id: build
        with:
          context: .
          file: docker/Dockerfile
          platforms: ${{ matrix.platform }}
          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}
          outputs: type=image,name=${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }},push-by-digest=true,name-canonical=true,push=true
          cache-from: type=gha,scope=${{ matrix.platform }}
          cache-to: type=gha,scope=${{ matrix.platform }},mode=max

      - run: |
          mkdir -p /tmp/digests
          platform="${{ matrix.platform }}"
          echo "${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}" > "/tmp/digests/${platform//\//-}"

      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: digest-${{ strategy.job-index }}
          path: /tmp/digests/*

  merge:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write

    steps:
      - uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          pattern: digest-*
          merge-multiple: true
          path: /tmp/digests

      - uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3

      - uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
        id: meta
        with:
          images: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}
          tags: |
            type=sha
            type=raw,value=latest,enable={{is_default_branch}}

      - run: |
          cd /tmp/digests
          tags=$(jq -cr '.tags | map("-t " + .) | join(" ")' <<< "$DOCKER_METADATA_OUTPUT_JSON")
          digests=$(cat * | xargs -I{} echo "${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}@{}")
          docker buildx imagetools create $tags $digests

file.content:
FROM debian:trixie-slim

ARG RTK_VERSION=0.42.4
ARG CODEX_VERSION=0.141.0
ARG GIT_WT_VERSION=0.29.0
ARG AGENT_BROWSER_VERSION=0.29.0
ARG TARGETARCH

ENV LANG=C.UTF-8
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# System packages
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    build-essential \
    ca-certificates \
    curl \
    git \
    grep \
    jq \
    locales \
    openssh-client \
    ripgrep \
    tree \
    unzip \
    zsh \
    zsh-autosuggestions \
    zsh-syntax-highlighting \
    && sed -i 's/^# *\(C.UTF-8\)/\1/' /etc/locale.gen \
    && locale-gen \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# GitHub CLI + Docker CLI
RUN curl -fsSL https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
      -o /usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
    && echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg] https://cli.github.com/packages stable main" \
      > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/github-cli.list \
    && curl -fsSL https://download.docker.com/linux/debian/gpg \
      -o /usr/share/keyrings/docker-archive-keyring.gpg \
    && echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/docker-archive-keyring.gpg] https://download.docker.com/linux/debian trixie stable" \
      > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/docker.list \
    && apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends gh docker-ce-cli docker-compose-plugin \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# starship prompt
RUN curl -fsSL https://starship.rs/install.sh | sh -s -- -y

# RTK
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) RTK_PKG="rtk_${RTK_VERSION}-1_amd64.deb" ;; \
      arm64) RTK_PKG="rtk-aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk/releases/download/v${RTK_VERSION}/${RTK_PKG}" -o "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}" \
    && if [ "${TARGETARCH}" = "amd64" ]; then \
         dpkg -i "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}"; \
       else \
         tar xzf "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}" -C /usr/local/bin; \
       fi \
    && rm -f "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}"

# agent-browser
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) AB_ARCH="x64" ;; \
      arm64) AB_ARCH="arm64" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser/releases/download/v${AGENT_BROWSER_VERSION}/agent-browser-linux-${AB_ARCH}" \
      -o /usr/local/bin/agent-browser \
    && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/agent-browser

# git-wt
RUN curl -fsSL "https://github.com/k1LoW/git-wt/releases/download/v${GIT_WT_VERSION}/git-wt_${GIT_WT_VERSION}-1_${TARGETARCH}.deb" \
      -o /tmp/git-wt.deb \
    && dpkg -i /tmp/git-wt.deb \
    && rm /tmp/git-wt.deb

# Scripts
COPY docker/bin/chrome-connect /usr/local/bin/chrome-connect

# Non-root user
RUN git config --system --add safe.directory /workspace \
    && useradd -m -s /bin/zsh agent
COPY docker/profile.sh /home/agent/.profile.sh
COPY docker/.zshrc /home/agent/.zshrc
RUN echo '. ~/.profile.sh' >> /home/agent/.bashrc
USER agent
WORKDIR /home/agent

# mise
RUN curl -fsSL https://mise.run | sh
ENV PATH="/home/agent/.local/bin:${PATH}"

# Claude Code
RUN curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash

# entire (AI session capture for git)
RUN curl -fsSL https://entire.io/install.sh | bash

# Codex
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) CODEX_ARCH="x86_64" ;; \
      arm64) CODEX_ARCH="aarch64" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/openai/codex/releases/download/rust-v${CODEX_VERSION}/codex-${CODEX_ARCH}-unknown-linux-musl.tar.gz" \
      -o /tmp/codex.tar.gz \
    && tar xzf /tmp/codex.tar.gz -C /home/agent/.local/bin \
    && mv /home/agent/.local/bin/codex-*-unknown-linux-musl /home/agent/.local/bin/codex \
    && rm /tmp/codex.tar.gz

# APM (requires python3)
USER root
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends python3 python3-pip \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
USER agent
RUN pip3 install --user --break-system-packages apm-cli

WORKDIR /workspace
CMD ["sleep", "infinity"]

## Context

Dockerfile にツールのバージョンが ARG で固定されている。これらを定期的に最新版へ更新し、PR を自動で出す CI を作る。

## 対象ツール

| ARG | リポジトリ | リリースタグ形式 |
|-----|-----------|----------------|
| `RTK_VERSION` | `rtk-ai/rtk` | `v0.42.4` |
| `CODEX_VERSION` | `openai/codex` | `rust-v0.141.0` |
| `GIT_WT_VERSION` | `k1LoW/git-wt` | `v0.29.0` |
| `AGENT_BROWSER_VERSION` | `vercel-labs/agent-browser` | `v0.29.0` |

## 方針

`.github/workflows/update-versions.yml` を新規作成。

- **トリガー**: `schedule` (週1, 月曜 9:00 JST = 日曜 0:00 UTC) + `workflow_dispatch`
- **処理**:
  1. 各リポジトリの最新リリースを `gh api` で取得
  2. Codex は `rust-v` プレフィックス付きタグなのでフィルタ
  3. 現在の Dockerfile ARG と比較し、差分があれば `sed` で更新
  4. 更新があれば `peter-evans/create-pull-request` で PR 作成
- **PR**:
  - ブランチ: `chore/update-tool-versions`
  - タイトル: `chore: update tool versions`
  - body にどのツールが何から何に更新されたか記載
  - 既存 PR がある場合は上書き更新

## 変更ファイル

- `.github/workflows/update-versions.yml` (新規)

## 検証

- `workflow_dispatch` で手動実行して PR が作られることを確認

| `AGENT_BROWSER_VERSION` | `vercel-labs/agent-browser` | `v0.29.0` |

## 方針

`.github/workflows/update-versions.yml` を新規作成。

- **トリガー**: `schedule` (週1, 月曜 9:00 JST = 日曜 0:00 UTC) + `workflow_dispatch`
- **処理**:
  1. 各リポジトリの最新リリースを `gh api` で取得
  2. Codex は `rust-v` プレフィックス付きタグなのでフィルタ
  3. 現在の Dockerfile ARG と比較し、差分があれば `sed` で更新
  4. 更新があれば `peter-evans/create-pull-request` で PR 作成
- **PR**:
  - ブランチ: `chore/update-tool-versions`
  - タイトル: `chore: update tool versions`
  - body にどのツールが何から何に更新されたか記載
  - 既存 PR がある場合は上書き更新

## 変更ファイル

- `.github/workflows/update-versions.yml` (新規)

## 検証

- `workflow_dispatch` で手動実行して PR が作られることを確認

input.content:
name: Update Tool Versions

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: "0 0 * * 0" # Weekly, Sunday 00:00 UTC (Monday 09:00 JST)
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: write
  pull-requests: write

jobs:
  update:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Check latest versions
        id: versions
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          get_latest() {
            gh api "repos/$1/releases/latest" --jq '.tag_name'
          }

          get_latest_by_prefix() {
            gh api "repos/$1/releases" --jq "[.[] | select(.tag_name | startswith(\"$2\")) | .tag_name][0]"
          }

          RTK=$(get_latest rtk-ai/rtk | sed 's/^v//')
          CODEX=$(get_latest_by_prefix openai/codex "rust-v" | sed 's/^rust-v//')
          GIT_WT=$(get_latest k1LoW/git-wt | sed 's/^v//')
          AGENT_BROWSER=$(get_latest vercel-labs/agent-browser | sed 's/^v//')

          echo "rtk=$RTK" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "codex=$CODEX" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "git_wt=$GIT_WT" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "agent_browser=$AGENT_BROWSER" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Update Dockerfile
        id: update
        run: |
          file="docker/Dockerfile"
          changes=""

          update_arg() {
            local arg="$1" new="$2"
            local old
            old=$(grep "^ARG ${arg}=" "$file" | cut -d= -f2)
            if [ "$old" != "$new" ] && [ -n "$new" ]; then
              sed -i "s/^ARG ${arg}=.*/ARG ${arg}=${new}/" "$file"
              changes="${changes}- **${arg}**: ${old} → ${new}\n"
            fi
          }

          update_arg RTK_VERSION "${{ steps.versions.outputs.rtk }}"
          update_arg CODEX_VERSION "${{ steps.versions.outputs.codex }}"
          update_arg GIT_WT_VERSION "${{ steps.versions.outputs.git_wt }}"
          update_arg AGENT_BROWSER_VERSION "${{ steps.versions.outputs.agent_browser }}"

          if [ -n "$changes" ]; then
            echo "has_changes=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            {
              echo "body<<EOF"
              echo -e "$changes"
              echo "EOF"
            } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - name: Create Pull Request
        if: steps.update.outputs.has_changes == 'true'
        uses: peter-evans/create-pull-request@v7
        with:
          branch: chore/update-tool-versions
          commit-message: "chore: update tool versions"
          title: "chore: update tool versions"
          body: |
            ## Updated versions

            ${{ steps.update.outputs.body }}

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: "0 0 * * 0" # Weekly, Sunday 00:00 UTC (Monday 09:00 JST)
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: write
  pull-requests: write

jobs:
  update:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Check latest versions
        id: versions
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          get_latest() {
            gh api "repos/$1/releases/latest" --jq '.tag_name'
          }

          get_latest_by_prefix() {
            gh api "repos/$1/releases" --jq "[.[] | select(.tag_name | startswith(\"$2\")) | .tag_name][0]"
          }

          RTK=$(get_latest rtk-ai/rtk | sed 's/^v//')
          CODEX=$(get_latest_by_prefix openai/codex "rust-v" | sed 's/^rust-v//')
          GIT_WT=$(get_latest k1LoW/git-wt | sed 's/^v//')
          AGENT_BROWSER=$(get_latest vercel-labs/agent-browser | sed 's/^v//')

          echo "rtk=$RTK" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "codex=$CODEX" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "git_wt=$GIT_WT" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "agent_browser=$AGENT_BROWSER" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Update Dockerfile
        id: update
        run: |
          file="docker/Dockerfile"
          changes=""

          update_arg() {
            local arg="$1" new="$2"
            local old
            old=$(grep "^ARG ${arg}=" "$file" | cut -d= -f2)
            if [ "$old" != "$new" ] && [ -n "$new" ]; then
              sed -i "s/^ARG ${arg}=.*/ARG ${arg}=${new}/" "$file"
              changes="${changes}- **${arg}**: ${old} → ${new}\n"
            fi
          }

          update_arg RTK_VERSION "${{ steps.versions.outputs.rtk }}"
          update_arg CODEX_VERSION "${{ steps.versions.outputs.codex }}"
          update_arg GIT_WT_VERSION "${{ steps.versions.outputs.git_wt }}"
          update_arg AGENT_BROWSER_VERSION "${{ steps.versions.outputs.agent_browser }}"

          if [ -n "$changes" ]; then
            echo "has_changes=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            {
              echo "body<<EOF"
              echo -e "$changes"
              echo "EOF"
            } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - name: Create Pull Request
        if: steps.update.outputs.has_changes == 'true'

toolUseResult.originalFile:
name: Update Tool Versions

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: "0 0 * * 0" # Weekly, Sunday 00:00 UTC (Monday 09:00 JST)
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: write
  pull-requests: write

jobs:
  update:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Check latest versions
        id: versions
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          get_latest() {
            gh api "repos/$1/releases/latest" --jq '.tag_name'
          }

          get_latest_by_prefix() {
            gh api "repos/$1/releases" --jq "[.[] | select(.tag_name | startswith(\"$2\")) | .tag_name][0]"
          }

          RTK=$(get_latest rtk-ai/rtk | sed 's/^v//')
          CODEX=$(get_latest_by_prefix openai/codex "rust-v" | sed 's/^rust-v//')
          GIT_WT=$(get_latest k1LoW/git-wt | sed 's/^v//')
          AGENT_BROWSER=$(get_latest vercel-labs/agent-browser | sed 's/^v//')

          echo "rtk=$RTK" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "codex=$CODEX" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "git_wt=$GIT_WT" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "agent_browser=$AGENT_BROWSER" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Update Dockerfile
        id: update
        run: |
          file="docker/Dockerfile"
          changes=""

          update_arg() {
            local arg="$1" new="$2"
            local old
            old=$(grep "^ARG ${arg}=" "$file" | cut -d= -f2)
            if [ "$old" != "$new" ] && [ -n "$new" ]; then
              sed -i "s/^ARG ${arg}=.*/ARG ${arg}=${new}/" "$file"
              changes="${changes}- **${arg}**: ${old} → ${new}\n"
            fi
          }

          update_arg RTK_VERSION "${{ steps.versions.outputs.rtk }}"
          update_arg CODEX_VERSION "${{ steps.versions.outputs.codex }}"
          update_arg GIT_WT_VERSION "${{ steps.versions.outputs.git_wt }}"
          update_arg AGENT_BROWSER_VERSION "${{ steps.versions.outputs.agent_browser }}"

          if [ -n "$changes" ]; then
            echo "has_changes=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            {
              echo "body<<EOF"
              echo -e "$changes"
              echo "EOF"
            } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - name: Create Pull Request
        if: steps.update.outputs.has_changes == 'true'
        uses: peter-evans/create-pull-request@v7
        with:
          branch: chore/update-tool-versions
          commit-message: "chore: update tool versions"
          title: "chore: update tool versions"
          body: |
            ## Updated versions

            ${{ steps.update.outputs.body }}
